# UC-17.2.20 · VPN Bandwidth Utilization Trending

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Beginner &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Performance, Capacity

*We watch how much data flows through our remote connection system over time, so we can plan ahead and add more capacity before things get too crowded.*

---

## Description

Time-series bandwidth per headend and user cohort — complements UC-17.2.7 top talkers with **trend** and **gateway** dimension.

## Value

Network capacity planners track VPN bandwidth consumption trends over time, correlating growing usage with session counts to forecast when WAN circuit upgrades or additional concentrators will be needed.

## Implementation

If bytes not in syslog, use SNMP interface counters or NetFlow `exporter=VPN`. Alert on sustained >80% of circuit.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (`sourcetype=cisco:asa`). Data in `index=vpn`.
- Key fields: `bytes_in`, `bytes_out`, `host` (concentrator hostname), `action=session_disconnect`.
- Supplementary: SNMP interface counters for the VPN tunnel interface, or NetFlow data.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify aggregate bandwidth data:
```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa" action="session_disconnect" earliest=-7d
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(bytes_in) as in_b sum(bytes_out) as out_b by _time, host
| eval total_gbps=round((in_b+out_b)/1073741824, 2)
| timechart span=1h sum(total_gbps) by host
```

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Bandwidth utilization trending:
```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa" earliest=-7d
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(bytes_in) as in_b sum(bytes_out) as out_b count as sessions by _time, host
| eval total_gb=round((in_b+out_b)/1073741824, 2)
| eval avg_per_session_mb=round((in_b+out_b)/max(sessions, 1)/1048576, 1)
| timechart span=1h sum(total_gb) as bandwidth_gb sum(sessions) as sessions
```

#### Understanding this SPL: Tracks aggregate VPN bandwidth and per-session averages. An upward trend in per-session bandwidth suggests users are performing more bandwidth-intensive tasks (video conferencing, large file transfers). An upward trend in total bandwidth with stable per-session bandwidth means more users are connecting. Both drive capacity planning.

### Step 3 — Validate
(a) Compare with SNMP interface counters for the VPN tunnel interface.
(b) On ASA: `show traffic` shows real-time throughput.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — "Total bandwidth (7d)", "Peak hour". Row 2 — Timechart: bandwidth and sessions.
Alert: Trigger when total bandwidth exceeds 80% of the WAN link capacity connected to the VPN concentrator.

Runbook:
1. **Bandwidth trending up** — Plan WAN circuit upgrade or VPN concentrator scale-out.
2. **Sudden spike** — Check for large file transfers or backup jobs. May need QoS policies.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- **Bandwidth data only at disconnect** — Normal for ASA. For real-time monitoring, use SNMP or NetFlow.
- **Totals don't match WAN monitoring** — VPN overhead (encryption headers) adds ~10-15% to raw payload.

## SPL

```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa" earliest=-7d
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(bytes_in) as in_b, sum(bytes_out) as out_b by _time, host
| eval gbps=round((in_b+out_b)*8/3600/1000000000,3)
| timechart span=1h avg(gbps) by host
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Line chart (Gbps per headend), Area chart (total VPN throughput), Table (peak hour).

## Known False Positives

Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.

- [Splunk_TA_paloalto](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757)
